title: Entity Storage Upsert and Select Pipeline
upsert_flow: START: Caller calls EntityStoreService.upsert(entity, id, options)
EntityStoreService reads entity by primary identifier → upstream.read(uid)
IF old_entity not found: check IdentifierUtil.detect_identifier(entity)
IF alternate identifier found: upstream.select({ predicate, limit:1 }) → set old_entity
IF still no old_entity: treat as INSERT (old_entity = null)
EntityStoreService calls upstream.upsert(entity, { old_entity, options }) → enters SubdomainES
SubdomainES.upsert(entity, extra):
IF extra.old_entity is null/undefined (new record): call _check_max_subdomains()
_check_max_subdomains: db.read('SELECT COUNT(id) AS subdomain_count FROM subdomains WHERE user_id=?', [user.id])
IF count >= max_subdomains (user.max_subdomains ?? config.max_subdomains_per_user):
THROW APIError('subdomain_limit_reached', { limit })
ELSE: continue
SubdomainES delegates to this.upstream.upsert(entity, extra) → enters SQLES
SQLES.upsert(entity, extra):
FOR each property with unique constraint: SELECT COUNT(*) to check uniqueness
IF count > 0: THROW APIError('already_in_use', { what, value })
IF old_entity exists: call update_(entity, old_entity)
update_: build UPDATE SET sql, db.write(), clone+apply old_entity → return { data, entity }
ELSE: call create_(entity)
create_: build INSERT INTO sql, db.write() → return { data, entity, insert_id }
END: return upsert result to caller
select_flow: START: Caller calls EntityStoreService.select({ predicate, limit, offset, params })
IF predicate is an Array: destructure [p_op, ...p_args]
upstream.create_predicate(p_op, ...p_args) → SubdomainES.create_predicate(id)
SubdomainES.create_predicate: IF id === 'user-can-edit': return new Eq({ key:'owner', value: Context.user.id })
ELSE delegate to upstream (SQLES) create_predicate
IF predicate is null after resolution: predicate = new Null()
EntityStoreService calls upstream.select({ predicate, ...rest }) → enters SubdomainES
SubdomainES.select(options):
Get actor from Context.get('actor'), extract user
Call svc_permission.check(actor, 'read-all-subdomains')
IF NOT has_permission_to_read_all:
options.predicate = options.predicate.and(new Eq({ key:'owner', value: user.id }))
SubdomainES delegates to this.upstream.select(options) → enters SQLES
SQLES.select({ predicate, limit, offset }):
IF predicate instanceof Predicate: call om_to_sql_condition_(predicate) → RawCondition
om_to_sql_condition_ handles: Null, And (recursive), Or (recursive), Eq, StartsWith, IsNotNull, Like
Build SQL: SELECT * FROM <table> [WHERE <condition>] [LIMIT n] [OFFSET m]
db.read(stmt, values) → rows[]
FOR each row: sql_row_to_entity_(row) → Entity (with property dereferencing, JSON parsing)
Return entities[]
BaseES.select (wrapper): IF read_transform defined: map entities through read_transform
EntityStoreService maps entities → entity.get_client_safe()
END: return client-safe entity array to caller
create_predicate_flow: Caller passes predicate as array e.g. ['user-can-edit']
Predicate used in subsequent select call with owner filter applied
key_conditions: UPSERT guard: old_entity null → check subdomain limit before insert
UPSERT guard: unique properties → SQL COUNT check before write
SELECT guard: missing 'read-all-subdomains' permission → force owner=user.id filter
SQLES: offset requires limit (else throws Error)
SQLES: delete throws entity_not_found if no rows affected
key_classes: SubdomainES extends BaseES — middleware: adds subdomain-specific auth/limit logic
BaseES extends AdvancedBase — base with METHODS static map, call_on_impl_ dispatcher, upstream chain
SQLES extends BaseES — terminal: all SQL operations (read/select/upsert/delete/create_/update_)
EntityStoreService extends BaseService — public API, predicate resolution, entity lifecycle
Eq/And/Or/Null/Like/StartsWith/IsNotNull extend Predicate — query DSL nodes
RawCondition — internal SQLES DTO: { sql: string, values: any[] }
key_relationships: SQLES depends_on Database service (DB_WRITE for writes, SubdomainES uses DB_READ for count check)
SQLES depends_on om (object model: properties, sql.table_name, primary_identifier)
important_notes: BaseES uses a middleware/chain-of-responsibility pattern. Each ES layer overrides only the methods it cares about; all others fall through to upstream via call_on_impl_. SubdomainES only overrides upsert, select, and create_predicate. SQLES is always the terminal layer with actual DB access. DB_READ used for count/select queries, DB_WRITE for mutations. Permission constant: 'read-all-subdomains'. Max subdomain config key: config.max_subdomains_per_user.
